claims.statement: DET8-GATE: peer balance commits (737acd6, 61a121c — stage1-rules-v2) consumed from origin/main in a dedicated worktree (branch cycle-008, per cycle-007 isolation verdict). game-core 14/14, playtest-5-stages PASS (S5 S,D,S VICTORY, settlement 2). Found+fixed on main: vertical-slice hardcoded stage1-rules-v1 in expectedRecords (broken by the peer's v2 bump) — now derives from the live core export, plus a NEW migration-guard test pinning RULES_VERSION===stage1-rules-v2 and proving a stale v1 record is rejected with reason RULES_VERSION.

Runtime/core convergence (advisory blockers): Stage 5 commands now route through the frozen reducer — each accepted command IS one semantic round (player effect + foe effect + round advance), the charge wrap is a pure telegraph (no damage, no round advance), passive focus regen is 0 on stage 5 (per-stage focusRegen presets), and STRIKE/BRACE/DISRUPT all pay commandCost() with disrupt_uses accounting. HOLD terminates naturally at round===schedule.length; records ARE the semantic game and finishEncounter's validateDeterministicReplay reports deterministic.

DET8-E2E: browser E2E exit 0 proving BOTH contracts with exact accepted-command streams — Campaign 1 (reactive, pinned D,D,B): Stage 5 HOLD, foe 3, focus 0.0 at terminal, commandLog exactly [DISRUPT,DISRUPT,BRACE], settlement '8 fragments' (HOLD award 0); Campaign 2 (deliberate trade S,D,S): Stage 5 VICTORY, foe 0, commandLog exactly [STRIKE,DISRUPT,STRIKE], settlement '10 fragments'. Escalating DISRUPT cost observed live (focus 4->3 then 3->1). Stages 1-4 remain reactive-winnable (VICTORY x4 in both campaigns).

DET8-DEPTH: Stage 2 feint (mid-lane 0.4s freeze, arrival schedule preserved: 3149ms vs 3200ms baseline), Stage 4 burst pair (0/450ms sub-waves, distinct lane heights), Stage 5 boss escalation (boss-aura-1 at foe<=3, boss-aura-2 at foe<=1, 4-unit pressure wave at foe<=1) — all presentation-only, null-guarded, reduced-motion safe; headless smoke evidence per item.

DET8-QA: shipped via push origin cycle-008:main (96c713a) without touching the peer's shared worktree; Pages workflow success for 96c713a; live app.js serves BUILD_TAG c008 with commandCost/reduceEncounter imports; live asset spot-check 200. capture-live exit 0; stage-5 victory terminal screenshot inspected (trade settlement 10 fragments, deterministic replay).

game-core.js untouched by this lane (peer-owned semantics consumed as-is). Stages 1-4 real-time loops keep their shipped endless-cycle behavior — bounded-schedule convergence was applied ONLY to stage 5 where the v2 economy demands it; extending it to stages 1-4 requires the peer's balance pass first (reserved as next action). No monetization/telemetry egress.

assumptions.statement: Stage-5 idle play (no command before charge wrap) is a telegraph-only pressure loop; the player is never damaged while idle on stage 5.
assumptions.validation_boundary: If playtests show idling feels consequence-free, route an idle-penalty rule through the peer's P2 balance pipeline — never as a runtime-only mutation.

assumptions.statement: Stages 1-4 sequences remain intentionally free-form (endless RT rounds), so their command logs are not contract-pinned.
assumptions.validation_boundary: When the peer extends v2-style bounded economies to stages 1-4, port the reducer-routing pattern and pin their logs the same way.

next_action.scope: Peer balance pass for stages 1-4 bounded economies (then reducer-route them like stage 5), APK build on Android-tooled hardware from the shipped TWA kit, and narration/HOLD-copy polish for the zero-award outcome
prohibited_work: game-core.js semantic changes in this lane
